title: Auth adapter — async auth_headers method on AuthProvider
purpose: Extend the AuthProvider trait with an async auth_headers method that extracts bearer token and chatgpt-account-id as header name-value pairs. The existing apply_auth method couples auth to reqwest::RequestBuilder; WebSocket handshakes need raw headers for the HTTP upgrade request. This brief provides that surface without touching the WS transport itself.
task: Add an async auth_headers() method to the AuthProvider trait. Implement it for OAuthAuthProvider (via AuthManager::auth().await), ApiKeyAuthProvider (bearer token only), and MockAuthProvider (consuming from the shared token sequence). The method returns Vec<(String, String)> of header name-value pairs.

requirements.title: AuthProvider trait gains auth_headers
requirements.spec: The AuthProvider trait SHALL define an async method auth_headers(&self) -> Result<Vec<(String, String)>, ProviderError>. The method SHALL return header name-value pairs suitable for an HTTP upgrade request.
acceptance: auth_headers method defined on AuthProvider trait
Return type is Result<Vec<(String, String)>, ProviderError>

requirements.title: OAuthAuthProvider implements auth_headers
requirements.spec: WHEN auth_headers is called on OAuthAuthProvider THE SYSTEM SHALL call self.manager.auth().await to retrieve the current token AND SHALL return Authorization: Bearer <token> and chatgpt-account-id headers.
acceptance: chatgpt-account-id header included when available from AuthManager
Returns ProviderError::AuthenticationFailed when no token is available

requirements.title: ApiKeyAuthProvider implements auth_headers
requirements.spec: WHEN auth_headers is called on ApiKeyAuthProvider THE SYSTEM SHALL return a single Authorization: Bearer <key> header AND SHALL NOT include chatgpt-account-id.
acceptance: ApiKeyAuthProvider::auth_headers returns one-element vec with bearer header
No chatgpt-account-id header in the result

requirements.title: MockAuthProvider implements auth_headers
requirements.spec: WHEN auth_headers is called on MockAuthProvider THE SYSTEM SHALL consume the next token from the same sequence used by apply_auth AND SHALL increment the shared apply_count.
acceptance: MockAuthProvider::auth_headers returns bearer header from token sequence
Calling auth_headers consumes the same token that apply_auth would have consumed

boundaries: SHALL NOT modify the existing apply_auth method signature or behavior
SHALL NOT modify the on_unauthorized method
SHALL NOT add WebSocket-specific code — this is a generic auth surface extension
verification: Existing apply_auth tests still pass unchanged
New tests verify auth_headers returns correct headers for each provider type
MockAuthProvider test verifies shared token sequence consumption
